intro: The clearest difference between Checkmarx and Private AI is pricing — Checkmarx is unknown (no free tier), while Private AI is usage-based. Beyond that, lock-in — Checkmarx has high lock-in (Medium-High — enterprise SAST, deep integration), whereas Private AI has medium lock-in (Usage-based PII redaction API). Checkmarx fits marketplace, apps; Private AI fits regulated industries and LLM apps needing PII redaction before storage, training, or inference. On the strengths side, Checkmarx is cited for UGC platforms (social, marketplace), while Private AI is cited for PII detection across modalities. The honest trade-off: Checkmarx is the wrong call for internal projects; Private AI is the wrong call for teams that just need toxicity, NSFW, or general UGC moderation; Checkmarx users flag B2B products without user content, and Private AI users flag not a general content moderation tool. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap.
quick_take: Checkmarx is for marketplace, apps; Private AI is for regulated industries and LLM apps needing; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Checkmarx if your use case is marketplace, apps, and a unknown (no free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than usage-based.
verdict_b: Choose Private AI if your use case is regulated industries and LLM apps needing PII redaction before storage, training, or inference, and a usage-based pricing shape fits your budget better than unknown (no free tier).

faq.q: Is Checkmarx cheaper than Private AI?
faq.a: Checkmarx is unknown, while Private AI is usage, so the cheaper option depends on your usage shape. Listed pricing pages usually change, so check both directly before committing. For small teams, the free-tier limits often matter more than headline prices.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Checkmarx to Private AI?
faq.a: Migrating from Checkmarx to Private AI is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Moving off Checkmarx is harder because of high lock-in (Medium-High — enterprise SAST, deep integration). Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

On our developer-experience score, Private AI is rated 4/5 and Checkmarx 3/5, so Private AI edges ahead. Scores like this smooth over real differences in docs, CLI feel, and SDK quality, so treat them as a starting point. Spend an afternoon in each before relying on the number.

faq.q: Is Private AI a good alternative to Checkmarx?
faq.a: Private AI is a reasonable alternative to Checkmarx when Private AI fits regulated industries and LLM apps needing PII redaction before storage, training, or inference. If your use case matches Checkmarx's sweet spot — marketplace, apps — staying with Checkmarx is probably safer. Checkmarx itself is not meant for internal projects, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
